Sir Gavin Williamson CBE Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Gavin Constituency News Campaigns In Parliament Get In Touch A Roadmap to Recognition Brewood Bridge Survey HS2 Survey Campaigns Stop Labour's tax on bus travel Stop Labour's 50% bus fare hike. Labour's plans to raise bus fares from £2 to £3 mean hard-working people commuting every day will pay £520 a year more. Sign... Stop the Family Farm Tax Labour have broken their promise to British farmers with the Family Farm Tax. Labour's Family Farm Tax will stop farms being passed to the next generation... Save our local pubs Labour's War on Pubs Will Close Your Local Labour's pub tax hike will force landlords to raise prices or shut their doors for good. £1,000s extra in business... Stop the grooming gangs It's time to get justice for victims. The only way to expose those who turned a blind eye to the grooming gangs is by holding a full inquiry, so sign our... Petition: Common sense immigration rules Our country is not a hotel, it is our home. This is our plan for immigration. We think: Newcomers should wait 10 years, instead of 5, before they can apply to... Employment Rights Bill Labour are making things even harder for businesses like yours. Their Employment Right Bill would mean: £5 billion in extra costs for businesses. Fewer jobs and... Petition: Save your Post Office Labour are threatening to close post offices across the country. Making it harder for people who are offline to access vital services. Reducing footfall to our... Say No to Starmer's ID Labour's “Digital ID” gimmick won't stop the boats. It's a desperate distraction from their scandals. We won't back any system that makes ID mandatory for... Scrap Stamp Duty on main homes Sign our petition urging Labour to scrap Stamp Duty on main homes. Cut my bills Labour promised to cut your energy bills by £300, but they’ve gone up by almost £200. Urge Labour to back our Cheap Power Plan which would cut everyone’s... Calling on the Government to scrap tax increases for farmers I am concerned about the Government’s plans to tax farmers leaving their farm to their family. This move would not affect “wealthy landowners”. In fact, the... What can I do for you?
